title: Empire Reborn
content:
{{Top|ffa|leg}} {{Otheruses|Empire}} {{Organization |image=[[File:Hethrir_newec.jpg]] |name=Empire Reborn |type=Imperial splinter faction''[[The Crystal Star]]'' |founder=Lord [[Hethrir]] |leader=*Lord Hethrir *[[Admiral/Legends|Admiral]] [[Galak Fyyar]] {{C|military}}{{Wizards|url=default.asp?x=starwars/article/sw20050407a|series=The Dark Forces Saga|text=Part 1}} |subgroup= [[Reborn]] |headquarters=''[[Rebirth]]'' |locations= |formed=[[Galactic Empire/Legends|Galactic Empire]] |founded=Some time during or after [[4 ABY/Legends|4 ABY]] |fragmented= |reorganized= |dissolved=[[14 ABY/Legends|14 ABY]] |restored= |affiliation= }} {{Quote|Today, I will be reborn—like the Empire, whose incarnation I have conceived and incubated. Today I will bring forth… the Empire Reborn.|Lord Hethrir, launching the Empire Reborn|}} The '''Empire Reborn''', also known as the '''Imperial Remnant''' (not to be confused with the mainline [[Imperial Remnant]] led by [[Gilad Pellaeon/Legends|Gilad Pellaeon]]), was a remnant of the [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Galactic Empire]] led by the [[Dark Jedi/Legends|Dark Jedi]] [[Hethrir]], the Imperial [[Procurator of Justice]] and a former student of the [[Sith Lord/Legends|Sith Lord]] [[Anakin Skywalker/Legends|Darth Vader]]. As a secret movement arising after the [[Battle of Endor/Legends|destruction]] of the second [[Death Star II/Legends|Death Star]] at [[Endor/Legends|Endor]], it infiltrated the [[New Republic/Legends|New Republic]] for a number of years as it built up its power base. Lord Hethrir recruited powerful allies to his cause including [[Admiral/Legends|Admiral]] [[Galak Fyyar]], former [[Jedi/Legends|Jedi]] student [[Desann]], and the shadowy [[Inquisitorius/Legends|Inquisitor]] [[Antinnis Tremayne]]. Financing its operations by slavery, the Empire Reborn trained a large army of Force-sensitive warriors that assaulted the [[Jedi Praxeum]] in [[12 ABY/Legends|12 ABY]]. After that invasion failed, the weakened movement kidnapped the [[Jaina Solo Fel|three]] [[Darth Caedus|Solo]] [[Anakin Solo|children]], in order to sacrifice them to the demigod [[Waru]], part of a sinister pact to gain absolute power in [[The Force/Legends|the Force]] for Hethrir. The combined efforts of the [[Jedi Knight/Legends|Jedi Knights]] [[Luke Skywalker/Legends|Luke Skywalker]] and [[Kyle Katarn]], as well as the [[New Republic/Legends|New Republic]] heroes [[Han Solo/Legends|Han Solo]] and [[Leia Organa Solo/Legends|Leia Organa]], foiled its plans, bringing about the movement's ruin. ==History== Lord Hethrir, a [[Firrerreo/Legends|Firrerreo]], was handpicked by the [[Sith Lord/Legends|Sith Lord]] [[Anakin Skywalker/Legends|Darth Vader]] to receive training as a [[Dark Jedi/Legends|Dark Jedi]]. Hethrir excelled in his training, and as a reward, was appointed [[Procurator of Justice]], head of the entire Imperial justice system. The role was an anonymous one, during which he exiled thousands of prisoners in sleeper-ships, and amassed a fortune from his exclusive ability to harvest [[body-wood]]. Before the Emperor was killed at the [[Battle of Endor/Legends|Battle of Endor]], he introduced Hethrir to his vision of a [[Dark Empire]] that he'd personally rule for a thousand years. In the [[Imperial Civil War|power vacuum]] that followed [[Endor/Legends|Endor]], Hethrir wasted no time putting a plan into motion. He formed alliances with his rivals [[High Inquisitor]] Tremayne, [[Admiral/Legends|Admiral]] [[Galak Fyyar]], and a number of wealthy Imperial nobles. Disgruntled Imperial lords, eager to regain their power and influence—such as the Lords [[Brashaa]], [[Cnorec]], and [[Qaqquqqu]], and Lady [[Ucce]]—joined him, swearing an oath of secrecy and devotion, bringing with them numerous followers and starships. Admiral Galak Fyyar was recruited to his cause in [[10 ABY/Legends|10 ABY]], financing many of his technological enterprises. Fyyar was known more for technological innovation than his skills as a military leader, but had worked closely with the Emperor before disappearing after his death. He consolidated their presence in the [[Outer Rim Territories/Legends|Outer Rim]], while alliances with [[Deep Core/Legends|Deep Core]] [[Warlord/Legends|warlords]] such as Admiral [[Blitzer Harrsk|Harrsk]] were pursued to strengthen their Core support. While protected by the anonymity of his former position, Hethrir was able to remain invisible to the [[Warlord/Legends|Imperial warlords]] and [[New Republic/Legends|New Republic]] while his [[Empire Reborn|remnant faction]] gained momentum. The years that followed were tumultuous, but nothing rivaled Hethrir's shock when he was summoned to the planet [[Byss/Legends|Byss]] to stand before the reborn Emperor Palpatine. Hethrir repledged his loyalty immediately, having understood Palpatine was not a man, but a vessel for the darkness of the universe, an avatar of the dark side itself. [[File:Galak43.jpg|thumb|left|200px|[[Admiral/Legends|Admiral]] [[Galak Fyyar]], the technical genius behind the Empire Reborn.]] When the Emperor [[Battle of Onderon (Galactic Civil War)|finally]] died, Hethrir believed that the dark side had now chosen him as its new champion. He believed he was destined to lead an Empire Reborn for the next millennium. It was at this point that he renamed his remnant faction to the Reborn Empire. Hethrir was ignorant to the [[Transfer essence|secret]] of [[Immortality/Legends|immortality]] and Palpatine's other god-like powers such as the abilitiy to create destructive [[Hyperspace/Legends|hyperspace]] [[Force storm (wormhole)|wormholes]]. If he was going to be the dark side's next champion, he knew he needed greater access to [[The Force/Legends|the Force]]. Sequestered alternatively on Crseih Station and his private [[worldcraft]], ''[[Rebirth]]''{{Hyperspace|url=hyperspace/member/insideronline/96/indexp2.html|series=Aliens in the Empire|text=Part 1: Hierarchy of Power}}—a unique gift from the Emperor himself—Hethrir built up a broad power base. Hethrir financed his movement with a flourishing slave trade, supplied by the thousands of prisoners he had condemned and imprisoned in sleeper ships, including his own people, the Firrerreo. He established Crseih Research Station—under the alias Asylum Station—as the hub of this slave trade. [[File:Desann-portrait.jpg|thumb|right|200px|[[Desann]], leader and trainer of the [[Reborn]].]] ===Creation of a Reborn army=== {{Quote|You are worthy to join my spearhead, worthy to join the Empire Youth.''"
"''You merit purification, I shall take you to be reborn in the service of the Empire.|Lord Hethrir promoting some of his agents|}} With these finances the Empire Reborn purchased armament from gangsters such as the [[Rodian/Legends|Rodian]] [[Reelo Baruk]], and, using armor, [[Deflector shield generator/Legends|shield generators]], and power cores scavenged from the derelict [[Star Destroyer/Legends|Star Destroyers]] orbiting [[Endor/Legends|Endor]],{{LucasArts|url=products/outcast/html/spin.html|text=N.R.I. Reports|int=N.R.I. Reports}} outfitted a fleet, with the ''[[Doomgiver]]'' as its flagship. Hethrir's military forces were led by Admiral Galak Fyyar. He came across the failed Jedi Knight, [[Desann]], a [[Chistori]] who had abandoned the [[Jedi Praxeum|Jedi Academy]] after killing a [[Havet Storm|fellow student]]. Fyyar recognized his potential and brought him to his obscure master. Despite an [[Humanocentrism|anti-alien bias]], Hethrir made Desann his right hand in the movement, entrusting him with the task of forming a Dark Jedi army. Searches for [[Force-sensitive/Legends|Force-sensitives]] on back water worlds such as [[Tatooine/Legends|Tatooine]] were begun. Those with potential were abducted and taken to ''Rebirth'' for indoctrination. Hethrir, with an ability to dampen others' Force abilities, would often prevent non-Humans from demonstrating any Force-ability, consigning them to be sold as slaves. Those Humans with Force-potential moved up the ranks of the [[Helpers]], [[Proctor (Empire Reborn)|Proctors]] and [[Empire Youth]]. Many of Hethrir's trainees were taken to the temple of the Waru on Crseih Station to be "purified". Waru was posing there as a healer, but occasionally killed beings brought for healing, sucking in their strength. Many of the Empire Youth that survived the purification were sent out into the Republic, working covertly to undermine it. Others were given to Desann to train. He took one of their number, a young Human female [[Tavion Axmis]], as his personal apprentice. Working with Galak on [[Cairn Installation]] in the [[Lenico Belt]], they experimented on Empire Youth with little talent for the Force, trying to artificially imbue them with dark Force powers via [[artusian crystal]]s and [[Sith alchemy/Legends|Sith alchemy]].''[[Star Wars: Jedi Knight II: Jedi Outcast]]'' It was at this time that the [[New Republic Intelligence Service]] first began to hear rumors of their activities, including several reports of Tavion's activity around the [[Lenico system/Legends|Lenico system]], and Imperial recruiters on Tatooine. Some preliminary investigations were begun but the extent of the threat not yet realized. [[File:Tremayne_MTSESB.jpg|thumb|left|200px|[[Antinnis Tremayne]], an ally of Lord Hethrir and of the Empire Reborn movement.]] In order to continue Galak's experiments, a [[New Republic/Legends|Republic]] mining facility on [[Artus Prime]] was captured in [[12 ABY/Legends|12 ABY]]. It had just discovered rich veins of artusian crystals, which the Empire Reborn confiscated, imprisoning the miners. These crystals were then shipped to [[Kejim]] for experimentation. The NRI intercepted garbled transmissions intercepted from Kejim containing the words "Reborn", and the "Valley of the Jedi", and sent [[Kyle Katarn]] and [[Jan Ors]] to investigate. [[Mission to Kejim|Razing]] the facility, the two commandos were then sent on to investigate the mine on Artus Prime. Desann, aware of the destruction of his Kejim facility and the [[Battle of Artus Prime|subsequent mission]], used it to set a trap for Katarn, another former Jedi. While Kyle investigated the mining facility, calling in a Republic task force to recapture the plant, Desann and his apprentice Tavion took Jan Ors prisoner. In a confrontation designed to humiliate and infuriate Kyle, Desann beat him and mocked his lack of Force ability—Kyle had let it atrophy during his time away from the Academy. Faking Jan's death, they escaped the planet before the Republic troops arrived, leaving Kyle a broken man. This was all a carefully calculated plan, designed to force Katarn into revealing the location of the famed [[Valley of the Jedi]], a [[Vergence/Legends|Force nexus]]. (Lord Hethrir had charged Desann with finding the Valley, after his ally, [[High Inquisitor]] Antinnis Tremayne, disclosed its existence.) Kyle duly obliged, traveling to [[Ruusan/Legends|Ruusan]] and restoring his own Force powers, swearing revenge on Desann. A [[Homing beacon/Legends|tracking device]] hidden in his ship, the ''[[Raven's Claw]]'', allowed Desann to follow after him. Desann then led his Empire Youth into the Valley's Force nexus, quadrupling their innate powers, and renaming them "[[Reborn]]." Galak's fruitful research produced a number of technological advances to aid the Reborn, including lightsaber-resistant [[Cortosis/Legends|cortosis]] armor, mined from the Lenico Belt, equipped with embedded artusian crystals aiding Force connection. Some of these troops were outfitted with stealth armor, forming an elite [[Shadowtrooper]] corps. [[File:ReeloBaruk.jpg||thumb|right|200px|[[Reelo Baruk]], [[Rodian/Legends|Rodian]] gangster and supplier to the Empire Reborn.]] ===Assault on the Jedi Praxeum=== {{Quote|I, Galak Fyyar the First, the genius who conceived of the Shadow Armor, will rule the galaxy with a Cortosis fist! Worlds will tremble, stars will shudder.|Galak Fyyar|}} Alerted to Desann's activities, and the threat to the Jedi, Kyle, Luke and the other Jedi Knights worked to undermine his activities. Luke visited the Valley of the Jedi and destroyed Desann's forces preventing him from creating any more Reborn. Kyle tracked Reborn activities to [[Nar Shaddaa/Legends|Nar Shaddaa]]. There, from [[Baruk's Bar]] he managed to infiltrate Reelo Baruk's operation, where, with the help of [[Lando Calrissian/Legends|Lando Calrissian]], he [[Mission to Nar Shaddaa (Empire Reborn)|neutralized]] the base, and killed the [[Crime lord/Legends|crime lord]]. Tracing the crime lord's operations to [[Bespin/Legends|Bespin]], Kyle and a Republic strike team further [[Battle of Bespin (Empire Reborn)|disrupted]] the Reborn's activities. In [[Cloud City/Legends|Cloud City]] Kyle encountered Tavion Axmis, confronting her in a lightsaber duel. Overpowering her, and tempted to kill her as revenge for the death of his partner Jan Ors, he [[Telekinesis/Legends#Force Choke|Force choked]] her. Afraid to die, Axmis revealed the ruse—Ors was still alive. Out of pity, Katarn let her live. She subsequently fled the Empire Reborn, afraid of reprisals from her master. Despite these set-backs, Lord Hethrir felt that with an army of Reborn, supplemented by Imperial [[Stormtrooper/Legends|Stormtroopers]], the time was right for a major confrontation with the Republic—an [[Battle of Yavin 4 (Empire Reborn)|invasion of the Jedi Praxeum]] on [[Yavin 4/Legends|Yavin 4]]. It would be a symbolic victory, destroying a symbol of hope and good in the Republic, and a powerful platform to launch the Empire Reborn into prominence. It would also be a personal assault, a chance for Desann to return to his former academy, showing them his true power. [[File:Apprenticeofdesann.jpg|thumb|left|200px|[[Tavion Axmis]], an [[Empire Youth]] and apprentice of Desann.]] The assault was to be launched from the Reborn's flagship, ''Doomgiver'', showering hundreds of [[All Terrain Rapid Deployment Pod]]s onto the moon. Each AT-RDP assault ship was to contain troops and [[All Terrain Scout Transport/Legends|AT-STs]]. However, the assault turned into an unmitigated disaster. Kyle Katarn had managed to [[Mission to Cairn Installation|infiltrate]] the ship just before it set out from Cairn Installation. During the [[Hyperspace/Legends|hyperspace]] leap, he confronted Galak Fyyar, who was armored in a unique shielded-suit, and killed him. Coming out of hyperspace, he rescued his partner Jan Ors, and alerted [[Rogue Squadron/Legends|Rogue Squadron]] to the ship's location. Bringing down the [[Deflector shield/Legends|shields]], he enabled the elite fighter squadron to destroy the ship, including many of the full-but-undeployed assault ships. This drastically cut the invasion force of the Empire Reborn. The Reborn already on the jungle moon, were soon outclassed by the more heavily trained Jedi. Escaping from the ''Doomgiver'', Kyle finally caught up to Desann in the heart of the [[Great Temple/Legends|Great Temple]]. Despite mutual attempts to turn their opponent, a duel ensued. With both combatants empowered by the Valley of the Jedi, they were closely matched but Katarn finally prevailed, killing Desann. The dramatic entry into galactic affairs envisaged by Lord Hethrir and his cohorts was in ruin. But even with his two main lieutenants killed, Hethrir was not prepared to give up on his dream; he would just have to resort to more drastic and radical plans. [[File:Doomgiver_Destroyed.jpg|thumb|right|200px|The ''[[Doomgiver]]'' explodes over [[Yavin 4/Legends|Yavin 4]].]] ===Sacrifice to the Waru=== {{Quote|We grow weary of concealing ourselves from usurpers of the New Republic. We must act soon, before our children are too much affected by egalitarian propaganda, before our generation is too old to act—to fight!|[[Brashaa]] complaining to Lord Hethrir|}} Despite pressure mounting from his supporters, and the huge investment into research and training lost, Lord Hethrir still had control of the younger Empire Youth and affiliated indoctrination programs. Having run out of freighter children to sell, he began to steal them from Republic worlds. As such, he was still able to supply Waru with Force-sensitives, and keep his bargain alive. With the Republic preoccupied by [[Gilad Pellaeon/Legends|Gilad Pellaeon's]] [[Orinda campaign]], Hethrir received a brief reprieve, reorganizing some of his facilities such as that on [[Alzoc III/Legends|Alzoc III]]. However, Kyle Katarn and the Republic continued to dog his steps, [[Mission at Alzoc III (Empire Reborn)|destroying]] that base, and killing some more of his precious few remaining Reborn.[[Star Wars: Jedi Knight II: Jedi Outcast demo|''Star Wars: Jedi Knight II: Jedi Outcast'' demo]]. The demo has conflicting information in it regarding whether it's set before, during or after the rest of the game. However, since Katarn has a lightsaber during the demo, this article places it after the full game where he regains his lightsaber. Rumors of abducted children also began to surface with Republic agent [[Winter Celchu|Winter]] sent out to investigate. [[File:Chewbacca_wounded.jpg|thumb|left|200px|[[Chewbacca/Legends|Chewbacca]] was wounded during the kidnapping of the Solo children.]] Still, Hethrir knew that if only he could get Waru to empower him, he could realize his vision. What he needed was a child exceptionally strong in the Force that would enable the Waru to return to his home dimension ''and'' allow Hethrir to receive the boundless power he craved. Hethrir believed the [[Solo family/Legends|Solo]] children would serve this purpose. Fortuitously, [[Chief of State (New Republic)/Legends|Chief of State]] [[Leia Organa Solo/Legends|Leia Organa Solo]] was undertaking a tour of the Outer Rim worlds soon after in [[14 ABY/Legends|14 ABY]], and was taking her children along with her. While [[Mon Calamari/Legends|Mon Calamari]] and [[Gbu]], the first planets on her itinerary would provide no opportunities, [[Munto Codru/Legends|Munto Codru]], the third planet on the tour, would provide the perfect arena for a kidnapping attempt. Its political system included ritualized "coup abductions" of noble children, who were later ransomed off unharmed. Hethrir correctly surmised that any pursuit or reprisals would be delayed under the assumption his kidnapping was only a part of the political sport. Temporarily blocking Leia's ability to sense her children, and using a [[pressure bomb]] to incapacitate the children's minders—the [[Wookiee/Legends|Wookiee]] [[Chewbacca/Legends|Chewbacca]] and a local page—[[Anakin Solo|Anakin]], [[Darth Caedus|Jacen]], and [[Jaina Solo Fel|Jaina Solo]] as well as the Munto Codru [[Chamberlain/Legends|chamberlain's]] [[wyrwulf]] were captured and whisked away by [[Skiff/Legends|skiff]]. They were shipped off planet to Hethrir's worldcraft, while Leia was still being advised to wait for a ransom request. [[File:Crystal_soldier.jpg|thumb|right|200px|[[Empire Youth]] guarded the Solo children on the worldcraft.]] On his worldcraft, Hethrir told the children their parents were dead and that he would look after them from now on as their [[Hold-Parent|hold-father]]. The children were suspicious, however, and proved remarkably resistant to the programming. Hethrir assigned his own son, [[Tigris]], to look after Anakin, and put the twins in with the rest of the imprisoned children, including a [[Chironian]] girl, [[Lusa]]. The kidnapped children caused much trouble at the station, using the Force to harass the young Proctors. Though held in separate cells, the Solo twins managed to escape their cells, taking a number of the other children with them. With the aid of a [[Mistress Dragon|female]] [[Sand dragon/Legends|sand dragon]] that Jacen communicated with, they escaped the entire compound and hid from the Proctors and Helpers in a swamp. Meanwhile, Leia and Chewbacca had been tracking Hethrir through the Force. They discovered a cluster of 24 sleeper ships, including one containing Hethrir's ex-mate, the [[Jedi healer/Legends|Jedi Healer]] [[Rillao]]. [[9 ABY/Legends|Five years earlier]], Hethrir had imprisoned her in a web like [[Torture/Legends|torture]] device on the ship. This ship was full of [[Firrerreo/Legends|Firrerreo]] that Hethrir had imprisoned as a sign of his loyalty to the Emperor, after which he [[Base Delta Zero/Legends|destroyed]] their entire [[Firrerre/Legends|world]]. Joining their party, Rillao led Leia and Chewbacca to the [[Unidentified boneless species|bureaucrats]] of [[Chalcedon/Legends|Chalcedon]]—a rival in the slave trade to Hethrir's own Asylum Station. Rillao revealed that Hethrir had stolen their son [[Tigris]] from her, hoping that he would succeed Hethrir as leader of his Empire Reborn. Upon realizing Tigris had no Force-potential, Hethrir had not even revealed his parentage to the boy, but kept him around as his personal servant. ===Crseih Station=== {{Quote|Waru, the time has come. You have Skywalker! Keep your promise to me, Waru. Make me omnipotent!|Lord Hethrir to Waru|}} Arriving at the worldcraft, Leia, Chewbacca, and Rillao rescued Jacen, Jaina and the other children in the [[Alderaan (yacht)|''Alderaan'']]. They were too late to rescue Anakin though, for Hethrir had already departed for Crseih Station with a phalanx of Proctors, intending to relaunch the Empire Reborn and sacrifice Anakin in the temple of the Waru. Following close on his heels, the two mothers pursued. [[File:Waru.jpg|thumb|left|200px|[[Waru]], a key component in Lord Hethrir's plan]] Also on Crseih Station, [[Han Solo/Legends|Han Solo]] and [[Luke Skywalker/Legends|Luke Skywalker]] were investigating reports of a lost Jedi, leaked by con-artist and former acquaintance of Solo, [[Xaverri]]. Xaverri had been on the station for a number of years and was deeply concerned about the "healer" Waru, and wanted them to investigate. Luke, however, seemed to be taken by the strange being, his judgment and Force ability crippled by the resonance of the nearby crystallizing star. He became distrustful of Han and Xaverri and convinced he needed Waru's help and healing. The [[Confrontation on Crseih Station|conflict]] came to a head, as the three parties met in the temple of the Waru. In a grand ceremony surrounded by all his supporters, Lord Hethrir planned to sacrifice Anakin on the altar of Waru. The newly arrived Rillao revealed to her son the truth about his father and, Tigris, realizing the infant Anakin had no choice in being "purified," turned on Hethrir, preventing the sacrifice. The unbalanced Skywalker dove into the Waru in Anakin's place, forcing Han and Leia to follow. Hethrir, realizing that Luke was a better sacrifice than Anakin anyway, believed his plans were finally coming to fruition. Rillao, however, launched herself onto Hethrir in a furious attack. Inside the golden ichors of the Waru, Han and Leia manage to draw Luke back, aided by the cries of the Solo children. Enraged by the missed opportunity and Hethrir's failure to fulfill his bargain, Waru consumed Hethrir instead. With Hethrir's Force power the being returned to its home dimension, presumably killing Hethrir. [[File:Luke_Crystal_Star.jpg|thumb|right|200px|[[Luke Skywalker/Legends|Luke Skywalker]] was weakened by the [[The Crystal Star|crystallizing star]]]] ===Aftermath=== {{Quote|Are you going to let a little thing like a rip through space-time slow you down?|Rillao to Luke Skywalker|}} With the crystallizing star dragged dangerously into the [[Black hole/Legends|black hole]], the entire Crseih Station was slaved to the '' [[Millennium Falcon/Legends|Millennium Falcon]] '' and together they jumped into hyperspace to the [[Codru system]]. The remaining Proctors and many of Hethrir's supporters were rounded up and arrested on the station. With Hethrir, Fyyar and Desann killed, the Empire Reborn movement collapsed. Aware of its existence, Leia pledged to find the remaining Empire Youth saboteurs. Some remained at large and eventually joined Desann's former apprentice Tavion Axmis, in a Sith cult, called the [[Disciples of Ragnos]]. The cult was later destroyed by the joint efforts of Luke Skywalker, Kyle Katarn, and his new apprentice [[Jaden Korr]].''[[Star Wars: Jedi Knight: Jedi Academy]]'' The Republic claimed the worldship and Crseih Station, and returned the other kidnapped children to their families. Some of the children, most notably the Chironian [[Lusa]], would go on to join the [[Junior Jedi Academy]] on Yavin 4.''[[Young Jedi Knights: The Emperor's Plague]]'' Crseih Station would eventually be moved to orbit the planet [[Pakuuni/Legends|Pakuuni]].''[[The Essential Guide to Planets and Moons]]'' Hethrir's slaveships were recovered and the Firrerreo people would eventually settle on the planet [[Belderone/Legends|Belderone]], made welcome by another former Dark Jedi, [[Flint]].[[The Essential Guide to Alien Species (real-world book)|''The Essential Guide to Alien Species'']] Hethrir's captured worldcraft would be studied by Republic engineers, including a former designer of the [[Death Star/Legends|Death Star]], [[Bevel Lemelisk]],[[The Essential Chronology (real-world book)|''The Essential Chronology'']] and the technology was later used in the [[New Class Modernization Program|New Class]] project. Finally, Hethrir's dream of a resurgent Empire would never be realized with the [[Imperial Remnant]] driven back to a paltry number of sectors in the Outer Rim. It would later sign a treaty with the Republic, the [[Pellaeon–Gavrisom Treaty|Bastion Accords]], bringing to an end the long [[Galactic Civil War/Legends|Galactic Civil War]].''[[Vision of the Future]]'' ==Behind the scenes== The connection between the Empire Reborn movement in ''[[The Crystal Star]]'' and Desann's Reborn Jedi from ''[[Star Wars: Jedi Knight II: Jedi Outcast]]'' was made in ''[[The Dark Forces Saga]], Part 6: Outcasts and Megalomaniacs Welcome''. [[File:Doomgiver4.jpg|thumb|right|200px|The ''Doomgiver'', loaded with [[All Terrain Rapid Deployment Pod]]s.]] ==Appearances== *''[[Star Wars: Jedi Knight II: Jedi Outcast]]'' {{Ret}} *[[Star Wars: Jedi Knight II: Jedi Outcast demo|''Star Wars: Jedi Knight II: Jedi Outcast'' demo]] {{Ret}} *''[[The Crystal Star]]'' {{1st}} *''[[Star Wars: Jedi Knight: Jedi Academy]]'' ==Sources== *[[Star Wars Galaxy Magazine 1|''Star Wars Galaxy Magazine'' 1]] {{C|Extract from ''The Crystal Star''}} *''[[The Essential Guide to Characters]]'' *{{GalaxyCite|8|The Deadly Dozen}} *''[[The Essential Guide to Planets and Moons]]'' *[[The Essential Chronology (real-world book)|''The Essential Chronology'']] *[[The Essential Guide to Alien Species (real-world book)|''The Essential Guide to Alien Species'']] *''[[The Dark Side Sourcebook]]'' *''[[The New Essential Guide to Characters]]'' *{{LucasArts|url=products/outcast/html/spin.html|text=N.R.I. Reports|int=N.R.I. Reports}} {{Ret}} *{{Wizards|url=default.asp?x=starwars/article/sw20050616a|series=The Dark Forces Saga|text=Part 6: Outcasts and Megalomaniacs Welcome}} *''[[The New Essential Chronology]]'' *{{Hyperspace|url=hyperspace/member/insideronline/88/indexp2.html|text=''Evil Never Dies: The Sith Dynasties''|int=Evil Never Dies: The Sith Dynasties}} *{{Hyperspace|url=hyperspace/member/insideronline/96/indexp2.html|series=Aliens in the Empire|text=Part 1: Hierarchy of Power}} *''[[Threats of the Galaxy]]'' *''[[The Complete Star Wars Encyclopedia]]'' *''[[Jedi Academy Training Manual]]'' ==Notes and references== {{Reflist}} {{GCW Reborn}} {{GalacCivilWarcampaigns}} {{Interlang |de=Neugeborenes Imperium#legends |es=Imperio Renacido |fi=Uudelleensyntynyt Imperiumi }} [[Category:Imperial splinter factions]] [[Category:Political factions]] {{RelatedCategories |[[Category:Empire Reborn astronomical objects]] |[[Category:Empire Reborn members]] }}
